grandpa.title: 👴 Tell Me a Story, Grandpa
grandpa.html:
'Grandpa, tell me a story about when you were young!' said Emma.

Grandpa smiled. 'When I was a boy, I arrived at school very early every morning. I walked two kilometres because there were no buses.'

'That is far!' said Emma.

'Yes! And after school, I cleaned the house and visited my grandmother. She lived in a small house near the river. She sailed a little boat on the river every day.'

'What about toys?' asked Emma.

'We didn't have many toys. We played outside with sticks and stones. I climbed trees and checked the clock to make sure I was home before dark.'

'Things were so different!' said Emma.

museum.title: 🏛️ In a Museum
museum.html:
Today the class visited a museum about life long ago. They saw many old things.

'Look at this old clock!' said Tom. 'It is very big.' 'And look at this old chair!' said Sara. 'People sat on chairs like this one.'

They saw an old lamp. It used oil, not electricity! They also saw an old bathrobe and a beautiful suit from a hundred years ago.

The teacher asked: 'How did people travel a hundred years ago?' 'They walked or used horses,' said Lucy.

'That's right! People arrived at places very slowly. Now we can travel fast. But some things are the same — people still love their families, just like then!'
